nodes.label: Supply Chain AI Agent System — replenishment to human-controlled.
Specialist agents (roadmap step 2: split into specialist agents — one job each).
Delivery / DC-planning agent — Plan Delivery. One job: schedule the inbound deli
Demand agent — Predict. One job: forecast demand from POS sales.
Evaluator agent — Evaluate the Workflow (roadmap step 7).  Checks the run for co
Exception agent — catch supplier delays / issues early. One job: flag exceptions
Rule-based monitor — fast, deterministic, no LLM needed for alerting triggers.
Inventory agent — Check Stock. One job: turn the forecast into a stock signal.
Procurement agent — Draft Order. One job: turn a stock signal into a draft order
Supplier agent — Pick Supplier. One job: choose the best supplier for the order.
Runtime configuration loaded from environment variables.
Mock 'real tools' — POS sales, inventory DB, supplier catalog, fleet schedule.
LangGraph orchestration — the supermarket supply chain as a chain of decisions.
Roadmap step 5 — humans approve risky actions; safe ones auto-approve.
Execute — issue the PO if approved, otherwise cancel.
Configurable LLM client with structured output.  Supports four providers via LLM
Return a validated `schema` instance.          `fallback` is a deterministic pro
FastAPI service exposing the supply chain agent graph.
Structured state passed between agents (roadmap step 4: pass structured state).
Every agent must explain its decision (roadmap step 6).
End-to-end CLI demo of the supply chain agent graph.  Usage:     python run_demo
Test that configuration loads correctly from environment variables.
Test that approval thresholds are properly typed as numeric values.
Test that the supply chain graph initializes without errors.
Test that demand forecast contains required fields.
Test that demand forecast confidence is low for SKUs with no sales history.
Test that demand forecast uses the correct horizon (7 days).
Test that stock signal has all required fields.
Test that suggested reorder quantity is always positive when reorder is needed.
Test that draft orders have valid urgency levels.
Test that procurement decisions always have a rationale.
Test that supplier choice has all required fields and valid types.
Test that supplier selection considers cost and lead time.
Test that delivery plan has valid date fields.
Test that delivery planning has justification.
Test that the decisions list accumulates from all agents.
Test that exceptions are properly captured in state.
Test that evaluation scores are between 0 and 1.
Test that high-cost orders require approval.
Test that final PO reflects the approval decision.
Test that cancelled orders don't have delivery ETAs.
Test that multiple SKU runs don't interfere with each other.
Test that different threads don't share state.
Test that the complete workflow runs without interruption or error.
Test that empty SKU is handled gracefully.
Test that unknown SKU is handled gracefully.
